name: OpenAI API
description: The incumbent first-party API; de-facto compatibility standard (Chat Completions / Responses)

provider.comment: OpenAI operates the API and trains/serves its own models.

category.comment: Trains and serves its own proprietary models (GPT-5.x, o-series, image/audio/embeddings) over its own API.

api-launched.comment: OpenAI's developer API first launched in private beta in June 2020; the Chat Completions endpoint followed in March 2023 and the Responses API in March 2025. Date from general knowledge; not re-verified against a primary source this pass.

status.comment: Developer API is generally available with published commercial pricing.

api-docs.comment: Official developer documentation and API reference.

website.comment: Official product page (not fetched; homepage returns 403 to automated fetch).

auth-methods.comment: Requests authenticate with a bearer API key; SDKs read OPENAI_API_KEY from the environment.

self-serve-signup.comment: Developers can self-serve create an account and generate an API key from the dashboard without sales contact.

free-credits-trial.comment: The fetched pricing page lists no standing free tier or trial credits. New accounts have historically received limited expiring credits and a data-sharing complimentary-daily-tokens program has existed, but neither could be confirmed from a primary source this pass (checked 2026-07).

regional-endpoints.comment: Default is a single global endpoint (api.openai.com); data residency/processing can be pinned per project to regions including the US, Europe, UK, UAE, Australia, Canada, Japan, India, Singapore, and South Korea (checked 2026-07).

data-residency-options.comment: Per-project data residency: at-rest storage across many countries and regional processing in the US, Europe, and UAE. Eligibility is sales-gated, not a self-serve toggle.

model-breadth.comment: Only OpenAI's own model families are reachable through the API.

open-weight-models.comment: The API serves OpenAI's proprietary models. OpenAI released gpt-oss open-weight models in 2025, but those are distributed for self-hosting rather than offered as a third-party open-weight catalog on this API.

model-version-pinning.comment: Dated immutable snapshots (e.g. gpt-4o-2024-08-06) can be pinned alongside floating aliases.

custom-fine-tuned-deployment.comment: A fine-tuning API lets customers train and then call their own fine-tuned models through the same inference endpoints.

model-catalog-size.comment: Dozens of distinct models across chat/reasoning, image (gpt-image, sora), realtime, speech/transcription, and embeddings families (checked 2026-07).

chat-messages-endpoint.comment: Offers both the Chat Completions messages endpoint and the newer Responses API for multi-turn inference.

streaming.comment: SSE token streaming; Responses adds semantic streamed events for tool usage.

tool-function-calling.comment: Documented function/tool calling in both Chat Completions and Responses, including strict-mode tool schemas.

parallel-tool-calls.comment: Model can emit multiple tool calls in a single turn (parallel_tool_calls, on by default).

structured-output.comment: Strongest guarantee is server-enforced Structured Outputs (strict JSON Schema); also offers plain JSON mode (response_format / text.format).

system-prompt.comment: Distinct system/developer role and instructions field supported at the API level.

image-input-vision.comment: Vision-capable models accept image input for reasoning.

audio-input.comment: Audio input via gpt-realtime and dedicated transcription models under the same API.

document-pdf-input.comment: PDF/document files can be provided as first-class input (file input / file_search) rather than pasted text.

image-generation.comment: Image generation endpoint via gpt-image models (and Sora for video) on the same account.

text-to-speech-audio-output.comment: Speech/audio synthesis output via TTS and gpt-realtime models.

embeddings-endpoint.comment: Text-embeddings endpoint (text-embedding-3 family) under the same API.

prompt-caching.comment: Automatic prompt-prefix caching (no caller-marked breakpoints); cached input billed at a steep discount (up to ~90% off on newer families).

batch-api.comment: Asynchronous Batch API completes within 24h at ~50% off standard pricing.

max-context-window.comment: GPT-5.6 flagship models (Sol/Terra/Luna) list a 1.05M-token context window; GPT-4.1 family is 1M (checked 2026-07).

extended-reasoning.comment: Reasoning models expose a reasoning-effort control and persist reasoning state across turns in the Responses API.

built-in-web-search.comment: First-party hosted web-search tool callable via the Responses API.

built-in-code-execution.comment: First-party hosted code-interpreter tool available via the Responses API.

mcp-support.comment: Responses API supports remote/hosted MCP connectors as a tool type.

files-retrieval-api.comment: Files API plus a hosted file_search retrieval tool the model can reference.

agents-assistants-api.comment: Responses API is stateful-by-default (conversations/tool state tracked automatically); the earlier Assistants API is being folded into it.

openai-compatible-api.comment: This is the reference OpenAI API itself — the de-facto compatibility standard other providers emulate.

official-sdks.comment: First-party SDKs: Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, .NET (with Microsoft), Ruby; Java and Go are in beta. PHP and Rust are community-only.

native-api-style.comment: OpenAI's own Chat Completions / Responses request shape is the native style.

webhooks-callbacks.comment: OpenAI provides webhooks for async event notifications (e.g. batch/realtime completion).

published-sla.comment: No standard contractual uptime SLA is published for the API; enterprise agreements may include a negotiated SLA. Could not confirm a specific figure from a primary source this pass.

rate-limit-model.comment: Fixed per-tier caps that auto-upgrade with cumulative spend (usage tiers), plus reserved capacity via Scale Tier / provisioned throughput.

provider-fallback-routing.comment: Single-vendor first-party API; no cross-provider routing/fallback.

no-training-default.comment: Data sent to the API is not used to train or improve OpenAI models unless the customer explicitly opts in to share it.

zero-data-retention.comment: Zero Data Retention available for approved/eligible organizations (excludes content from abuse logs, forces store=false); default abuse-monitoring retention is up to 30 days.

compliance-certifications.comment: SOC 2 Type 2 for the API Platform, ISO/IEC 27001, GDPR support with DPA, and HIPAA BAAs on ZDR-eligible endpoints. FedRAMP/PCI not confirmed for the standard API this pass.

enterprise-sso-access-control.comment: Org-level access controls / RBAC and workload identity federation; SSO/SAML available for enterprise accounts.

pricing-model.comment: Primarily per-token usage pricing (Standard/Batch/Flex/Priority tiers); reserved throughput available via Scale Tier.

unified-billing.comment: One OpenAI account/bill covers all models and endpoints.

cache-batch-discount.comment: Batch API ~50% off; cached input billed at a large discount (up to ~90% off on newer model families).

cheapest-entry.comment: Lowest-friction paid start is self-serve pay-as-you-go per-token usage.
